# @andes/popper

Popper is essentially a card which overlaps or "floats above" the content of an UI. It's the first step towards having a more unified implementation of componentes with similar behavior, i.e.: a Tooltip's message or a Dropdown's List.
> This is an internal component, if you need to use it please contact us to follow the use case.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/popper
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/popper/index';
```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Popper } from '@andes/popper';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Popper](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/popper--andes-popper).

### Basic Example

```jsx
<Popper content={<div>This is the content, could be another component or an HTML node</div>}>
  <button>This is a custom trigger</button>
</Popper>
```

### With child component Example

For more information about this case, check out the [component children documentation](https://github.com/atomiks/tippyjs-react#component-children).

```jsx
const Component = ({ref, ...props}) => {
  return <button ref={ref}>Reference</button>;
};

<Popper content={content}>
  <Component>
</Popper>
```

## Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom trigger. It's the node which opens the popper. | `node` | `undefined` | |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `content` | Content within the popper. | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `id` | Unique identifier. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `interactive` | Allows the component to be interactive, to hover over and click inside it. | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `onClickOutside` | Callback fired when the user clicks anywhere outside of the popper. | `func(instance, event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onHidden` | Callback fired when the popper closes. | `func(instance, { preventFocus: boolean })` | `undefined` |  |
| `paddingSize` | The padding size of the popper. | `number: 0, 16, 24, 32` | `0` |  |
| `placement` | Position of the popper relative to the trigger. This can be `bottom`, `bottom-start`, `bottom-end`, `top`, `top-start`, `top-end`, `left`, `left-start`, `left-end`, `right`, `right-start`, `right-end`. | `string` | `bottom` | |
| `popperOptions` | Specifies custom popper options. This gives you full control over the component positioning. See [Popper's docs](https://popper.js.org/docs/v2/) for details. | `object` | `undefined` |  |
| `style` | Style object for popper card. | `object` | `undefined` |  |
| `trigger` | Event or events that could open the popper. For example we could use `'mouseenter'`, `'click'`, `'focusin'`, `'focus'` or any combination of those options (only for uncontrolled mode). | `string` | `'click focus'` |  |
| `visible` | Controlled mode. | `bool` | `undefined` |  |
